mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5 percent in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and consumer credit outstanding indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and financing conditions, which historically precedes sharp equity drawdowns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.5 percent, aggressive federal funds rate hikes fail to stabilize equity valuations because real rates remain negative, causing sustained downward pressure on the S&P 500 over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly negative when CPI exceeds 3.5% and FEDFUNDS rises, or the signal produces positive returns in > 55% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which directly supports equity earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When new housing starts fall below 1.2 million units annually, it signals a rapid cooling in the construction sector and broader consumer confidence, which typically leads to negative equity returns over the following quarter as growth expectations adjust downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while consumer credit contracts indicates a supply-driven economic expansion financed by inventory drawdowns rather than sustainable demand, which historically triggers mean-reverting equity corrections over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid labor market cooling where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0 percent, the combination of weakening demand and restrictive policy historically causes sharp equity drawdowns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE rises >0.4pp and FEDFUNDS >4.0%, or the signal produces positive returns in > 55% of triggered windows.
